objective: Create a ‘Bird and the Balloons’ project using the code blocks.

concept_definitions.definition: Sprite Lab is a block-based programming environment where you can make simple animations and games with objects and characters that interact with each other. Create an animation or game then share your app in seconds.

warmup_qa.a: Sprites are the characters or animations that we add in the project.

warmup_qa.q: How can we reduce the problem of writing the same code multiple times?
warmup_qa.a: We can make use of the loops.

warmup_qa.q: What was your favorite part of creating our project?
warmup_qa.a:
Repeat loop.
What are sprite behaviors?[2 marks]Answer: Behaviors are pre-defined sets of actions that a sprite can perform over and over.
 Where did we store the game points? [2 marks]
Answer:In a score variable.
What are the different types of events added to move the bird  ? [2 marks]
Answer: Key pressed events.
(This is an assessment to recall the concepts learnt so far. Follow the checkpoint after this).

Evaluate the understanding / learning level of the student.
If the Score is between 0-3 then he/she is a slow learner. Explain the concepts again according to Assessment 1 and then  go to this slide.
       If the Score is between 4-5 then the learner is a medium learner. Keep working on
       the activities in natural flow. Go to this slide.
       If the Score is between 6-8 then the learner is a fast learner. Keep working on

### Notes:

<!-- Slide number: 23 -->

Fun Activity 1

![](GoogleShape827p38.jpg)
It’s time to refresh ourselves with a cool activity! I have a
“Turtle Wax”  for you!
(Share the link with the student & Ask the student to open the link).

Click and drag the turtle to adjust the slide. Release to send the turtle down the slide.

Adjust the height of the ladder to try to land on the target. Land on the target three times to win!

### Notes:

<!-- Slide number: 24 -->

Coding the App

Code Blocks when Birdie touches GreenBallooon

When the birdie touches green balloon, increment the score by 1.
Play a bubble pop sound
Display which color balloon is  popped !
Display the score also.
Make the balloon jump to random locations.

### Notes:

<!-- Slide number: 27 -->

Assessment 2

Ask the student the following

checkpoint_qa.q: Which loop is used in the project  ?

checkpoint_qa.a: Behaviors are pre-defined sets of actions that a sprite can perform over and over.

checkpoint_qa.q: Where did we store the game points?
What are the different types of events added to move the bird  ?

common_errors: Forgetting to add behaviors to sprites
Forgetting to initialise the variable before using it
Not using repeat block for multiple sprites
Setting sprite size before creating the sprite
